title: Functional Workflow Walkthrough
scope: Local-only walkthrough packet for proving the Guided AI Labs Operations Cockpit and CRM Command Center path with a harmless internal example. This file does not connect to Microsoft 365 and does not perform tenant writes.

sampleScenario.name: Internal Guided AI Labs readiness walkthrough
sampleScenario.description: Use one internal, non-client, non-external example to prove the operating path from intake signal through CRM engagement, decision, delivery action, and handoff evidence.
allowedData: Internal Guided AI Labs test organization/contact names
Links to existing internal pages or placeholder evidence
Clear labels that records are internal walkthrough artifacts

safetyLimits: No tenant writes are performed by the Stage 8D packet generator or preflight
No permissions, sharing, guests, app grants, public Forms, mail sends, deletes, or unattended automation
Manual walkthrough writes, if Adam approves them in browser, must stay internal and reversible
Stop if the Operations Cockpit or CRM Command Center is confusing, missing, or shows the wrong daily path
Stop if any step requires client/external data before the workflow is approved

workflowSteps.operatorAction: Open the live homepage and confirm the CRM, Operations, Tools, Projects In Flight cards and embedded attention queues are understandable.
workflowSteps.acceptance: Adam can identify the CRM Command Center as the single daily CRM door without using the older reference pages.

workflowSteps.targetSurface: Guided AI Labs - Intake Register / Attention Now
workflowSteps.operatorAction: Create or identify one internal test intake row labelled with the walkthrough record prefix.
workflowSteps.expectedRecord: Internal intake signal with owner, status, next action, and durable note
workflowSteps.evidenceTarget: Intake row link or browser note
workflowSteps.acceptance: The signal appears in the intake queue and has a clear next action.

workflowSteps.operatorAction: Create a qualification row linked to the internal scenario and choose a recommended package.
workflowSteps.expectedRecord: Qualification row with status, fit score, urgency, recommended package, owner, and next action due date
workflowSteps.evidenceTarget: Qualification row link or browser note
workflowSteps.acceptance: The row appears in Qualification Triage until qualified, disqualified, nurture, or converted.

workflowSteps.targetSurface: CRM - Organizations, Contacts, Engagements, Stakeholder Map, Touchpoints
workflowSteps.operatorAction: Create the minimum internal organization/contact/engagement/touchpoint records required to represent the scenario.
workflowSteps.expectedRecord: Organization, contact, engagement, stakeholder role, and first touchpoint records with RecordKey/CentralOSLink/GraphNodeId left blank or clearly placeholder
workflowSteps.evidenceTarget: CRM record links or browser notes
workflowSteps.acceptance: The engagement carries entry package, target package, engagement stage, execution stage, owner, next action, and migration hooks.

workflowSteps.sourceSurface: Decision Register and CRM - Action Queue
workflowSteps.targetSurface: Decision Register, CRM - Action Queue, Agent Action Log
workflowSteps.operatorAction: If the scenario requires an internal operating decision, record it; then create one action queue item and one agent/evidence log note.
workflowSteps.expectedRecord: Decision row only if there is a real internal decision; action queue row with due date and owner; Agent Action Log evidence
workflowSteps.acceptance: The action appears in Open CRM Actions and any decision has an explicit rationale.

workflowSteps.sourceSurface: Active Delivery page and Planner/List operating surfaces
workflowSteps.targetSurface: CRM - Action Queue, Lifecycle Checklist, Handoff Packet Register
workflowSteps.operatorAction: Confirm the scenario can move from qualified/onboarding into active delivery without losing owner, status, blockers, or evidence.
workflowSteps.expectedRecord: Lifecycle checklist item or delivery action with owner, blocker state, and evidence requirement
workflowSteps.evidenceTarget: Checklist/action/handoff packet link or browser note
workflowSteps.acceptance: A future staff member can see what must happen next without asking where the record lives.

workflowSteps.sourceSurface: CRM - Artifacts / Handoff Evidence and Client Handoff Packets
workflowSteps.targetSurface: CRM - Artifacts, Handoff Packet Register, Readiness Evidence
workflowSteps.operatorAction: Record a harmless placeholder evidence artifact and confirm it shows in the handoff/evidence queues.
workflowSteps.expectedRecord: Artifact or handoff packet row labelled internal walkthrough, with no public/client sharing
workflowSteps.acceptance: The operating path ends with a durable evidence pointer and no external exposure.

stopGates.trigger: Adam cannot tell which page/list should be used next from the cockpit or CRM Command Center.
stopGates.response: Stop and refine navigation/page copy before creating Teams tabs or automation.

stopGates.trigger: A record could belong in client tenant, Guided AI Labs tenant, OneDrive draft space, or SharePoint official record space and the owner is unclear.
stopGates.response: Stop and record the ownership decision before continuing.

stopGates.trigger: The walkthrough appears to require guest access, external sharing, a public Form, or an email send.
stopGates.response: Stop. Treat it as a Stage 7/9 approval-gated decision, not a Stage 8D walkthrough action.

stopGates.trigger: A decision, action, handoff, or artifact cannot be linked back to the source signal.
stopGates.response: Stop and add the missing evidence link before adding more records.

reviewQuestions: Can Adam open the cockpit and know what to do first?
Does the CRM Command Center make actions, qualification, meetings, artifacts, and health visible without extra explanation?
Can one internal example move from intake to handoff evidence without touching external sharing or email?
Are the old Relationship CRM and CRM Operations pages clearly reference pages rather than daily doors?
What is the first place where the system still feels confusing, duplicative, or too manual?

captureFields.purpose: Date/time the browser walkthrough was performed.
Browser/profile used for the walkthrough, for example the work tenant profile.
Internal dummy record prefix used during the run.
Pass, needs refinement, blocked, or skipped.
Internal SharePoint/List/page link or a short browser note.
What felt confusing, duplicative, missing, or too manual.
Specific improvement, decision, or stop-gate action.
